pi_items.source: Task README and task.json specify this as the target vehicle profile name to be created. Sygic GPS Navigation allows custom vehicle profile names.

Sygic public documentation (https://www.sygic.com/what-is/vehicle-profiles and https://www.sygic.com/enterprise/gps-navigation-for-trucks-vans-and-fleets) confirms VAN as a supported vehicle type option. Multiple tasks in this environment (configure_delivery_vehicle, overnight_refuse_collection using TRUCK and BUS) confirm these are database enum values.
Sygic public documentation (https://www.sygic.com/what-is/vehicle-profiles) confirms DIESEL as a supported fuel type for vehicle profiles. Multiple tasks use DIESEL fuel type consistently.
Sygic public documentation confirms production year is a configurable vehicle profile field. Year 2021 is a valid, recent year. Euro 6d became mandatory for all new registrations on January 1, 2021.

Sygic public documentation (https://www.sygic.com/blog/2019/avoid-emission-zones-that-are-restricted-for-your-vehicle-type) confirms Euro emission standards (1-6) are supported. Wikipedia and EU sources (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/European_emission_standards) confirm Euro 6 is a real, official EU emission standard introduced in 2014, with Euro 6d becoming mandatory January 1, 2021.

Task setup_task.sh and README confirm the pattern: '0' means Shortest route, '1' means Fastest route. Sygic documentation (https://www.sygic.com/what-is/route-computing) confirms three route computing options: Fastest, Shortest, and Economic. However, the specific numeric encoding (0=Shortest, 1=Fastest) is not documented in publicly available Sygic documentation or SDK references. The encoding is internally consistent within the task system: setup sets it to '1' (fastest), agent must change to '0' (shortest), export reads it back, verifier checks for '0'.

Sygic public documentation (https://www.sygic.com/what-is/route-computing) explicitly lists toll roads as an avoidable road type. The setup script sets this to 'false' (allowing tolls), and the task requires the agent to set it to 'true' (avoiding tolls). The string values 'true'/'false' are standard boolean representations in preferences files.

Task setup_task.sh and README confirm the pattern: '0' means Miles, '1' means Kilometers. Sygic documentation (https://www.manula.com/manuals/sygic/sygic-gps-navigation/1/en/topic/regional) confirms distance units are configurable between metric (km) and imperial (miles). However, the specific numeric encoding (0=Miles, 1=Km) is not documented in publicly available Sygic documentation. The encoding is internally consistent within the task system.

Sygic SDK documentation (https://developers.sygic.com/maps-sdk/changelog/25.7/) confirms 'arriveInDrivingSide' as an official routing option. The feature allows routing to arrive at a destination in the correct direction (pulling up on the correct side of the street). The setup script sets this to 'false', and the task requires setting it to 'true'. String values 'true'/'false' are standard boolean representations.

privileged_info_summary: The courier_urban_delivery task configures Sygic GPS Navigation (com.sygic.aura) on Android to set up a delivery van driver's device. Sygic supports all claimed vehicle profile attributes: vehicle types (car, van, truck, bus, motorhome), fuel types (including diesel), and emission categories (Euro 1-6 standards per EU regulations). The app offers three route computing methods: fastest (least time), shortest (minimum distance), and economic (lowest fuel consumption). Route planning includes toll road avoidance and arrive-in-direction features (ensuring drivers pull up on the correct side of the street). Production year 2021 is valid for Euro 6d vehicles (mandatory from Jan 1, 2021). The metadata for vehicle_name, vehicle_type (VAN), fuel_type (DIESEL), production_year (2021), emission_category (EURO6), avoid_toll_roads (true), and arrive_in_direction (true) are all consistent with Sygic's documented capabilities. The numeric encodings for route_compute (0=Shortest) and distance_units (1=Km) cannot be independently verified from public Sygic documentation, though they are internally consistent within the task system's scripts and verifier.
